pi_items.source: verifier.py and export_result.sh explicitly check for >= 3 web attack rules.
Task description requires severity level 10 or above for SQL injection. export_result.sh extracts the sql_level.
Task description requires severity level 10 or above for path/directory traversal. export_result.sh extracts traversal_level.
Task description requires severity level 9 or above for command injection. export_result.sh extracts cmd_level.
Task description requires MITRE mapping. verifier.py checks for 'has_mitre_mapping' and awards 15 points.

pi_items.metadata_value: Create nginx decoder and web attack detection rules with MITRE ATT&CK mapping
pi_items.verified_value: Create nginx decoder and web attack detection rules with MITRE ATT&CK mapping
pi_items.source: Summarizes the explicit instructions found in task.json description and setup_task.sh.

privileged_info_summary: I have verified that the task operates on a synthetically reset Wazuh configuration via setup_task.sh without external real-world log data. The task instructions accurately require creating at least 3 custom rules (SQL injection at level 10+, path traversal at level 10+, and command injection at level 9+) and a MITRE ATT&CK mapping. While export_result.sh explicitly extracts these individual rule severity levels, I noted that the scoring logic in verifier.py is lenient, only requiring the maximum severity across all created rules to be >= 10 to award full severity points.
